subject: First Aid & Safety
title: The Danger Zone Temperature
description: A quick win: understand The Danger Zone Temperature.

hook.text: Between 40°F and 140°F, bacteria on food double every 20 minutes. It's called the danger zone.

buildup.text: Food left in this temperature range for over 2 hours becomes a bacterial breeding ground.

discovery.text: Refrigeration slows bacterial growth. Cooking above 165°F kills most dangerous organisms.

twist.text: You can't see, smell, or taste dangerous bacteria—contaminated food often looks perfectly normal.

climax.text: A food thermometer is more reliable than cutting into meat to check if it's 'done.'

punchline.text: Below 40 or above 140. Those numbers protect you. Memorize them.

quiz.question: What is the food 'danger zone' temperature range?
